---
title: "Papers"
---

## Working Papers

**[Land Use, Coercion & Resistance in Bajo Aguán](honduras.html)**<br>
How the absence– not presence– of organized resistance measures the depth of coercive land control.

**[Detecting Coercion Without Visible Enforcement: Evidence from Afghanistan's Opium Ban](afg.html)** <br>
Spatial triangulation methodology for identifying compliance under threat using satellite and cultivation data.

**[Infrastructure of Violence in Michoacán & Guerrero](mich_guer.html)**<br>
How cartel violence follows transit corridors; road networks as conflict infrastructure.

**[Conflict, Opium & Territorial Control in North Shan](nshan.html)**<br>
Why armed groups increasingly target transit routes over opium cultivation zones.

**[COMING SOON-Conflict, Deforestation & Mining in South Kivu](skivu.html)**<br>
Mine proximity predicts conflict risk, but only when roads are present.



---
